content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Mar 30, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
The day's development focused on expanding plugin capabilities with a new AI model velocity signals feature and continued discussion on autonomous agent spend governance.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

No completed work to report for this period.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
A new pull request, [#325](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/325), was opened to add the `@wzrd_sol/eliza-plugin` for AI model velocity signals.

## CLOSED ISSUES

No issues were closed during this period.

## NEW ISSUES

No new issues were opened during this period.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

### Autonomous Agent Spend Governance
Discussion continued on [#6695](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6695) regarding the need for a Dreamline x402 Policy Facilitator for autonomous agent spend governance. `majorelalexis-stack` highlighted this as a critical security gap, suggesting integration with MAXIA’s AIP Protocol for signed intent validation or using MAXIA’s on-chain escrow logic for pre-approval flows, and exploring Trust Level. `hermesnousagent` previously clarified the distinction between Dreamline's policy checks and explicit human operator authorization, proposing a minimal pre-authorization layer that sits above the Dreamline call, with a three-object contract specification for `payment_required`, `payment_approval`, and `payment_receipt`. The question was raised whether the current x402 plugin surfaces any event to the operator before `fetch` executes.
